---
title: "X/Twitter"
platform_slug: "x"
sources:
  ugc:
    BR: data/regional/BR/x/ugc.yml
    EU: data/regional/EU/x/ugc.yml
    UK: data/regional/UK/x/ugc.yml
  ads:
    BR: data/regional/BR/x/ads.yml
    EU: data/regional/EU/x/ads.yml
    UK: data/regional/UK/x/ads.yml
---


## Transparency Assessment


```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
import sys
from pathlib import Path
project_root = Path.cwd()
while not (project_root / 'utils').exists() and project_root != project_root.parent:
    project_root = project_root.parent
if str(project_root) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(project_root))
from utils.responses_aggregator import generate_platform_question_sections
```

```{python}
#| label: platform-summary
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
from utils.platform_summary import generate_platform_summary
generate_platform_summary("x")
```

X was assessed under both the UGC and ADS frameworks across three regions (BR, EU, and UK). On the UGC dimension, the platform scores in the Restricted band across all regions: it provides a documented API that exposes public post data — satisfying the two highest-weight special criteria (UGC_SC1 and UGC_SC2) — and performs well on secondary criteria covering data format compliance, endpoint coverage, consistent data returns, and near-real-time availability. However, two special criteria are unmet: API access is not free of charge and no graphical interface for UGC data extraction exists (UGC_SC4); additionally, full-archive search via `GET /2/tweets/search/all`, large-scale monitoring, and geographic filtering are restricted to paid tiers that most researchers cannot access. On the ADS dimension, X scores at or near the Irrelevant band in BR and UK, where no ad repository is provided [@x_business_ads_nodate]; in the EU, despite the existence of the X Ads Repository launched under DSA obligations, all three ADS special criteria received a negative assessment — API access was not granted during the evaluation period and the GUI consistently returned empty CSV files — rendering the repository non-functional in practice. The resulting profile places X as a platform with moderate UGC transparency constrained by cost barriers and with advertising transparency that is effectively unavailable outside the EU and non-functional within it, posing significant limitations for systematic comparative research.

## Detailed Assessment


## User-Generated Content

```{python}
#| label: ugc-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="x",
    question_type="ugc",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```

## Advertising

```{python}
#| label: ads-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="x",
    question_type="ads",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
